Name: Hemoglobin
Description: The oxygen-carrying protein in red blood cells that gives blood its red color
Fun Fact: First protein structure solved by X-ray crystallography, earning Max Perutz the Nobel Prize

Name: Insulin
Description: Hormone that regulates blood sugar levels in the body
Fun Fact: First protein to have its amino acid sequence determined by Frederick Sanger in 1951

Name: Green Fluorescent Protein (GFP)
Description: Jellyfish protein that glows green under UV light, revolutionized cell biology
Fun Fact: Won the 2008 Nobel Prize and is now used in millions of experiments worldwide

Name: DNA Double Helix
Description: The iconic B-form DNA structure that stores genetic information
Fun Fact: Watson and Crick built their model using X-ray data from Rosalind Franklin

Name: SARS-CoV-2 Spike Protein
Description: The protein that COVID-19 uses to enter human cells
Fun Fact: Structure was solved in just 12 days after sequence release - a scientific record

Name: p53 Tumor Suppressor
Description: Guardian of the genome - prevents cancer by stopping damaged cells
Fun Fact: Mutated in over 50% of all human cancers, making it the most studied protein in cancer research

Name: CRISPR-Cas9
Description: Revolutionary gene-editing tool derived from bacterial immune systems
Fun Fact: Led to the 2020 Nobel Prize, fastest ever from discovery to Nobel

Name: Myoglobin
Description: Oxygen-storing protein in muscles that makes meat red
Fun Fact: The very first protein structure ever solved at atomic resolution

Name: Collagen
Description: Most abundant protein in your body, forms skin, bones, and tendons
Fun Fact: Makes up 30% of all protein in your body and is the main ingredient in gelatin

Name: Deoxyhemoglobin

Name: Lysozyme

Fun Fact: Discovered by Alexander Fleming before penicillin, first enzyme structure solved

Name: Trypsin
Description: Digestive enzyme that breaks down proteins in your intestine
Fun Fact: One of the first enzymes discovered, in 1876 by Wilhelm Kühne

Name: ATP Synthase

Name: Alcohol Dehydrogenase

Name: Hen Egg White Lysozyme

Fun Fact: Has been crystallized more times than any other protein

Name: Streptavidin
Description: Bacterial protein with the strongest non-covalent bond in nature
Fun Fact: Biotin-streptavidin bond is used in countless lab techniques

Name: Reverse Transcriptase

Name: Cytochrome c

Name: Acetylcholinesterase

Name: CD8 T Cell Receptor

Name: Tumor Necrosis Factor

Name: Interleukin-2

Name: Interferon-gamma

Name: Complement C3

Name: C-Reactive Protein

Name: Ras GTPase

Name: Src Kinase

Name: Protein Kinase A
